coverage.degradation: prior submission failed validation: dossier.pivots[1] missing provenance; this resubmission reduces to a single, fully-provenanced pivot entry rather than attempting to repair the unrecoverable second entry

aliases: MSD (Merck Sharp & Dohme, used outside the US and Canada)

value.founding_story: Merck & Co. began as the US subsidiary of the German company E. Merck, established in New York in 1891; it was seized and sold as an independent American company by the US government's Alien Property Custodian during World War I, becoming fully independent of the German parent in 1919.

value.indication: metastatic melanoma / multiple oncology indications (PD-1 inhibitor)

value.name: Robert M. Davis
prior: Merck Executive Vice President and CFO

value.from: Merck's 2020 10-K described a broadly diversified business spanning Pharmaceutical, Animal Health, and Women's Health/Biosimilars/Legacy Brands segments as core to its strategy
value.to: Merck spun off its women's health, legacy brands, and biosimilars businesses into a standalone independent public company, Organon & Co.
value.trigger: activist and investor pressure to focus capital allocation on the high-growth oncology and vaccines pipeline following Keytruda's commercial success
evidence: Merck press release announcing the Organon spin-off completion, 2021-06-02
value.outcome: Merck has not re-entered the women's health/legacy brands business through 2026; Organon has traded as an independent NYSE-listed company (OGN) since June 2021

value.detail: Merck discontinued development of two COVID-19 vaccine candidates, V590 and V591, after Phase 1 studies showed immune responses inferior to natural infection and to other approved vaccines; the company pivoted resources toward its therapeutic candidate molnupiravir.
value.program: Merck's COVID-19 vaccine candidates V590 and V591
